nodes.label: AI as enhancer of human voice and authenticity
nodes.label_negative: AI as diluter of personal tone and authenticity
nodes.grounding_span: I usually have to edit these email responses to ensure that they firstly sound like they have come from a human, and secondly have the exact tone that it looks and feels like it has come from me.

nodes.label: AI as strategic overview generator
nodes.grounding_span: I sometimes find the responses slightly generic, but they tend to give a good overview

nodes.label: AI as iterative collaborator requiring human guidance
nodes.grounding_span: I never delegate complete tasks, I like to provide an overview of the work I have done first and ask for opinions, improvements or a starting point. I then make edits, send it back through the AI and work in an iterative pattern.

nodes.label: AI as productivity multiplier for time-consuming tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as substitute for core understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I am very happy for AI to do them jobs that I feel take a lot of time, such as refining and editing emails and grants.

nodes.label: AI as threat to jobs lacking deep understanding
nodes.label_negative: AI as safe tool for those with expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I do think that it is a threat to a large number of very specific jobs.

nodes.label: AI as continuous consultant with broad knowledge
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for specific precision tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine I will more or less use AI as a continuous consultant, which has a much broader knowledge and understanding than I do in most topics.

nodes.label: Authentic human connection and rapport
nodes.grounding_span: building a relationship with another human, who you are likely to interact with in the real world, should be built on true human-human interactions.

nodes.label: Deep understanding and expertise
nodes.grounding_span: it is important to have a strong understanding of the tasks that are being carried out

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I am very happy for AI to do them jobs that I feel take a lot of time

nodes.label: Strategic control and business success
nodes.grounding_span: I expect it will help our company keep our team small and agile for longer.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for creative visual tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I felt particularly satisfied when I used AI to create a visual representation of a LinkedIn post that I wanted.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI image generation precision
nodes.grounding_span: I have been frustrated at the image generation quality and lack of ability to follow simple requests

nodes.label: Frustration with AI handling large information
nodes.grounding_span: The AI fails to handle such large quantities of information, and the outcome becomes quite tangential to the initial prompt.

nodes.label: Disapproval of colleagues' heavy AI reliance
nodes.grounding_span: I do not particularly like that they rely heavily on AI, as I feel I send them a brief that I have spent a good quantity of time focusing on, and they run it through an AI to create their ideas.

nodes.label: Optimistic vision of AI as continuous consultant
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine I will more or less use AI as a continuous consultant

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with human-in-the-loop
nodes.grounding_span: I then make edits, send it back through the AI and work in an iterative pattern.

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting for AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I do not use AI to make final decisions, to make legal decisions, or to advise on safety regulations relating to the product.
